title: transport: REQUEST_ID global shared across RelayConn instances; response routing ambiguous with multiple connections
description: next_request_id() is a process-global AtomicU64. When a process creates multiple RelayConn instances, all share the same ID space. This is unique but if code tries to match a request-response pair across connections using the ID alone, it can be confused. Document that request IDs are process-global and per-connection response routing is the caller's responsibility.
